exhausted_directions: postflop fold gate/threshold tuning in should_fold_postflop()
layering additional fold gates (parallel or sequential)
replacing equity estimation method inside fold logic
direction_audit.mandatory_constraints: DO NOT modify should_fold_postflop(), add new fold gates, adjust fold thresholds, or change equity estimation inside fold logic. The bot has had 0% postflop fold rate across 4 consecutive generations despite repeated fold-gate modifications — the problem is UPSTREAM. Instead, target one of: (1) Preflop range tightening — the bot defends too wide preflop, creating unplayable postflop hands that cannot fold; (2) Street-by-street pot-control — reduce calling frequency at flop/turn BEFORE the river decision; (3) Build a new opponent action-sequence classifier (e.g., passive-line vs aggression-line detector) that feeds INTO existing decision logic without touching fold gates. The constraint is: zero edits to should_fold_postflop() or any function whose name contains 'fold'.
direction_audit.suggested_direction: Investigate WHY the bot reaches postflop with unplayable hands: audit preflop defense ranges and limp/call thresholds. If the bot defends 95%+ of hands preflop, fold logic is irrelevant — widen preflop fold ranges first. Alternatively, add a flop/turn street-level 'give-up' action (check-fold) in strategy.py's action selection, independent of should_fold_postflop().
